title: Git Remotes & Sync
description: Learn to manage remotes, fetch updates, pull changes, push branches, and inspect remote-tracking branches.

tasks.explanation: git remote -v shows remote names and their fetch/push URLs.

tasks.prompt: Add a remote named 'origin' pointing to 'https://github.com/user/repo.git'.
tasks.explanation: git remote add registers a named connection to a remote repository.

tasks.prompt: Fetch all updates from the 'origin' remote without merging.
tasks.explanation: git fetch downloads remote commits and updates remote-tracking branches.

tasks.prompt: Pull changes from 'origin/main' into the current branch.
tasks.explanation: git pull fetches and merges remote changes into the current branch.
git push publishes local commits to the remote repository.

tasks.prompt: Push branch 'feature' and set upstream tracking to 'origin/feature'.
tasks.explanation: -u sets the upstream so future git pull/push use the remote branch.
git branch -r shows branches available on configured remotes.

tasks.prompt: Show detailed information about the 'origin' remote.
tasks.explanation: git remote show displays URLs and tracked branch configuration.
